MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES via CourtCall:
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant and moving party Riverside Acoustics, Inc.
Mia Mattis of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1338, X-134), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Jeffrey Martin Hammer.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and Court rules on its oral tentative ruling: Defendant Riverside Acoustics, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiff's claims for false representation and punitive damages is GRANTED. As to Plaintiff's cause of action for punitive damages, Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's interrogatory responses nos. 29-33 provide no facts and/or simply provides laundry lists of people and/or documents. Plaintiff's request for a continuance of the motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(h) is denied. Defense counsel stated that the only remaining corporate representative for Riverside Acoustics Inc. passed away in September 2021. The current preferential trial date of December 20, 2021 does not allow sufficient time for the education of a new PMQ/COR. Both sides submitted to the Court's tentative ruling in this matter. Court to issue and serve order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Jeanese Johnson, CSR #11635, Jeanese@verizon.net (Tel: 310-703-2537) (503/CML)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Matthew Goldberg of DLA PIPER (US), LLP (215-656-3377), appearing for defendant and moving party Dr. Ing. H.C.F. Porsche AG.
Madeline L. Buty of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant Porsche Cars North America.
Lafayette J. Clarke of POND NORTH LLP (213-617-6169), appearing for defendant Genuine Parts Company.
Katherine C. Elford of McDERMOTT WILL & EMERY (310) 277-4110, appearing for defendant Honeywell International, Inc.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Specially Appearing Defendant Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG's Motion to Quash on the Grounds of Improper Service and Lack of Jurisdiction is GRANTED. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice of Exhibits 21 and 24-28 is DENIED.

Plaintiff has failed to meet his burden of showing that service on PCNA was effective service on Porsche AG as CT Corporation is not the agent for service of process for Porsche AG. Further, Plaintiff has failed to satisfy the requirement of Code of Civil Procedure section 416.10(b) for service on Porsche AG. Even after being given the opportunity to conduct jurisdictional discovery, Plaintiff has failed to present sufficient evidence that PCNA is Porsche AG's "general manager." Yamaha Motor Co. v. Superior Ct. (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 264, 274 is distinguishable given Porsche AG's corporate structure under which PCNA is separated from Porsche AG by Porsche Enterprises Incorporated, PPF Holding AG of Zug, Switzerland and Porsche Nordamerika Holding GmbH of Ludwigsburg, Germany. (See also Cosper v. Smith & Wesson Arms Co. (1959) 53 Cal. 2d 77, 83 ["every object of the service is obtained when the agent served is of sufficient character and rank to make it reasonably certain that the defendant will be apprised of the service made"].)

Plaintiff has also failed to produce admissible evidence showing that the Court has specific jurisdiction over Porsche AG. Plaintiff's exhibits 1-30 are stricken based on hearsay and lack of foundation. Plaintiff further violated this Court's August 26, 2021 order limiting discovery to jurisdictional discovery only. Even so, Plaintiff provides no evidence that Porsche AG purposefully directed activities to California during the relevant time period between 1972 and 1974. Plaintiff's request for further jurisdictional discovery is denied. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Specially Appearing Dr. Ing. H.C.F. Porsche AG's Motion to Quash on Grounds of Improper Service and Lack of Jurisdiction is continued to October 14, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503 so that Plaintiff may conduct jurisdictional discovery. Plaintiff's objections to paragraphs 5, 8, 9 and 12 to the declaration of George Feygin are sustained based on lack of foundation. Supplemental opposition, if any, is due by September 27, 2021. Supplemental reply, if any, is due by October 4, 2021.
